description: You are a historical novelist researching the night sky conditions during the RMS Titanic disaster. Using the coordinates provided in /home/ga/data/historical_events.json (41.726°N, 50.233°W, Altitude 0m), simulate the view on April 15, 1912, at 02:20 UTC. The display must show an open-ocean nighttime view with the atmosphere, constellation lines, and constellation labels enabled, and the ground/landscape disabled so no terrain obstructs the sky. Capture screenshots of the Moon, Polaris, and Jupiter centered in the view, saved to /home/ga/Pictures/stellarium/. Record your research findings, including the year '1912' and whether the 'moon' was visible, in /home/ga/Desktop/titanic_sky_research.txt.

metadata.data_source: British Wreck Commissioner's Inquiry (1912) for distress signal coordinates
